page_content:
Formerly: KNES498T.

KNES306 Prosthetics for Limb Amputations (3 Credits)

Introduction to the science of prosthetics and the artificial devices that are designed, developed and fit to replace the missing body part or parts lost through trauma, disease or congenital conditions.

Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in BSCI201, BSCI202, and KNES300. Restriction: Must be in a major within the SPHL-Kinesiology department; and must have earned a minimum of 75 credits.

Credit Only Granted for. KNES306 or KNES498W.
